# Here are the 6 most popular majors in COMP110, with the number of students in each:
<img src="https://grahamnoah7777.github.io/grahamnoah7777.github.io-repository2/static/imgs/top_majors.png" alt="6 top majors and number of students. "  width="500"/>

# Here is a comparison between the number of students in the top 6 majors (first dict) and the number of students in both of Dr. Hinks's sections:
<img src="https://grahamnoah7777.github.io/grahamnoah7777.github.io-repository2/static/imgs/compare.png" alt="Comparison between top 6 majors and total. "  width="500"/>



Based on the data, about 2/3rds of students in Dr. Hinks's section fall into one of 6 majors. Because of this, it would be advantageous to those students for COMP110 instructors to implement elements of those most popular majors into some of the exercises, in order for most students to learn about practical applications of computer science in their chosen field of study. This would benefit students by causing them to be more engaged in the exercises.
